title: Identify easily people who click on the url in mail sent via Monday

posts.author: brianfromfrance (S)
posts.cookedHtml:
As I talked with Luna from Monday yesterday, thanks to her btw.
I need a new automation from the monday team if it’s okay for you, it will be great !!!
There is already an automation from Emails & Activity called “when an email is opened, set status to something”.
I wanted to get a new automation “when an url is clicked on an email, set status to something”.
I guess it is a small thing to do for devellopers as you have already an automation similar
thanks for your help

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: @brianfromfrance I wonder would this be a Feature Request? Feature requests - monday Community Forum

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: This doesn’t fix your specific use case, but if you use mass email feature, you can see who clicks on the links. https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/10989655392914-Email-campaigns-and-mass-emailing

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml: @brianfromfrance until that will be developed by monday, I can suggest u to use a marketplace app named SuperMail which offers this functionality

posts.author: brianfromfrance (S)
posts.cookedHtml:
Noam Lapidot:

SuperMail

Thanks for your kind answer, but we should pay for this app… I hope Monday can handle more automation on their own (free for their customer) as it’s not a service on outside like connect Calendly or other stuff, it’s on their sheets that we want them to work
